summary: REQ-FEED-030 EARS guards on both `editingStatus ∈ {editing,saving,switching,save-failed}` AND `editingNoteId === self.noteId`, but the Acceptance Criteria only check one variable at a time. The case `editingNoteId === self.noteId AND editingStatus === 'idle'` has no AC.

detail.snippet:
EARS L994: WHEN viewState.editingStatus ∈ {'editing','saving','switching','save-failed'} AND viewState.editingNoteId === self.noteId THEN ... mount BlockElement.

AC L1014: viewState.editingNoteId === self.noteId のとき, FeedRow の DOM ツリーに ... が blocks.length 個存在する.
AC L1015: viewState.editingNoteId !== self.noteId のとき ... 0 個.

context: AC L1014 omits the `editingStatus` half of the guard. If `editingNoteId === self.noteId` AND `editingStatus === 'idle'` (semantically possible for the brief moment after `select_past_note` and before `editing_session_state_changed` arrives, or after a DomainSnapshotReceived that resets the editing slice), the EARS says BlockElement should NOT mount, but the AC says it SHOULD. Implementation could legitimately interpret the AC and produce a spec-violating BlockElement mount.
recommendedFix: Rewrite AC L1014/1015 as a 2x2 truth table over (editingStatus, editingNoteId): (1) editingStatus ∈ {editing,saving,switching,save-failed} AND editingNoteId === self.noteId → BlockElement count = blocks.length; (2) editingStatus === 'idle' AND editingNoteId === self.noteId → 0; (3) editingStatus !== 'idle' AND editingNoteId !== self.noteId → 0; (4) editingStatus === 'idle' AND editingNoteId !== self.noteId → 0. Add a corresponding integration test case for (2).
